question_text: A perfume bottle is opened in one corner of a room. After some time, the smell reaches the other side of the room. Using diffusion and the particle model, explain how this happens and why it takes longer in a cold room than a warm room.
correct_answer: Perfume particles evaporate into gas particles that spread throughout the room by diffusion. They move from high concentration (near bottle) to low concentration (rest of room). In a cold room, particles move slower and collide more, so diffusion is slower. In a warm room, particles have more energy and move faster, so they spread out and mix more quickly.
explanation: This question tests understanding of how particles move and spread (diffusion), and how temperature affects particle motion. In a warm room, particles move faster so the smell spreads quicker. In a cold room, particles move slower so it takes longer for the smell to reach the other side. This is multi-step because it combines diffusion, evaporation, and temperature effects.
